bay-county.public_access: The interactive CityView locator is CAPTCHA-gated, but Bay County also publishes public permit report PDFs on its permits page.
notes: Official permit search still runs through CityView and now prompts for CAPTCHA at the public locator, which makes direct live scraping brittle.
Bay County publishes monthly permit report PDFs with permit type, permit number, address, owner, contractor, issued date, status, and valuation.

The live adapter now consumes those public report PDFs and filters to PRSF one-and-two-family-dwelling permits with issued dates, which is a better fit for pace tracking than the CAPTCHA-protected locator.

panama-city.public_access: Panama City's public Cloudpermit map is available without login and is backed by searchable public-map API calls.
notes: Panama City publicly documents that permit applications route through Cloudpermit, and the public map for domain group US-FL54700 is reachable without authentication.
The public map issues requests to public-map/config-v2 and public-map/search-v2, which can be replayed with Transit request bodies while asking for JSON responses.

The live adapter now sweeps the municipality map tiles, deduplicates results by municipal case id, and filters to likely new residential dwelling permits so Panama City can participate in the same pace-tracking workflow as the other jurisdictions.

madison-county-al.public_access: Madison County's CityView permit search requires login. The authenticated permit locator is the only path to countywide PRBD results; without credentials the adapter has no usable public surface.
notes: Adapter is currently a stub — fetch_permits() raises NotImplementedError until CityView credentials are provisioned.
Target portal is CityView (Harris) at https://cityview.madisoncountyal.gov/Portal. Login at /Portal/Account/Login.
Target flow once credentials exist: authenticated PRBD year search at /Portal/Permit/Locator, following links to permit-status detail pages.
Required env vars when implementing: MADISON_AL_CITYVIEW_EMAIL, MADISON_AL_CITYVIEW_PASSWORD.

Do NOT conflate with the Madison County probate portal at madisonprobate.countygovservices.com (Azure AD B2C + Kendo Grid, documented in AL-ONBOARDING.md) — different vendor, different auth, different data.

okeechobee.public_access: Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal with public REST API for permit search by date range. No authentication required.
notes: Okeechobee County transitioned from TRAKiT/eTRAKiT to Tyler Technologies EnerGov Civic Access in June 2025.
The public Civic Access portal exposes a three-endpoint REST API: tenant init, criteria template, and paginated search.
The live adapter searches by issue date range, filters to residential new-construction permit types, and maps CaseNumber, AddressDisplay, IssueDate, CaseType, CaseStatus, MainParcel, and ProjectName.

hernando-county.public_access: Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal with public REST API for permit search by date range. No authentication required.
notes: Hernando County uses the same Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal pattern as Okeechobee.
The public API uses the same three-endpoint flow: tenant init, criteria template, paginated search.

marion-county.public_access: Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal with public REST API for permit search by date range. No authentication required.
notes: Marion County uses the same Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal pattern, hosted at a custom domain (selfservice.marionfl.org).
The public API uses the same three-endpoint flow: tenant init, criteria template, paginated search.

walton-county.public_access: Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal with public REST API for permit search by date range. No authentication required.
notes: Walton County uses the same Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal pattern.
The hostname uses a hyphen: waltoncountyfl-energovweb (not waltoncountyflenergovweb).
The public API uses the same three-endpoint flow: tenant init, criteria template, paginated search.

desoto-county-ms.public_access: Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal with public REST API for permit search by date range. No authentication required.
notes: DeSoto County, Mississippi uses the same Tyler EnerGov Civic Access portal pattern, hosted on the county's own domain.
This is a Mississippi county, not Florida â€” state is MS.
The public API uses the same three-endpoint flow: tenant init, criteria template, paginated search.

polk-county.public_access: Polk County publicly routes permitting through an Accela Citizen Access portal that supports anonymous search by address, parcel number, permit number, contractor, and record type.
notes: The official Polk County Building service page links directly to the Polk County Access Portal for online permitting.
The county's Accela user guide documents public search by address, parcel number, permit number, contractor, and record type without requiring a logged-in session.

The live adapter uses the public Building search for Residential New permits, fetches record detail pages for parcel/job-value metadata, and splits date windows to stay below the apparent 100-result grid cap.

panama-city-beach.public_access: Public permit and inspection search is documented and exposed through an iWorQ portal.
notes: The official Panama City Beach page lists the public search criteria and links directly into the iWorQ permit search.
The iWorQ page renders public result grids and inspection details, making this the strongest candidate for a future live adapter.
The current implementation now uses the public date-range search with searchField=permit_dt_range plus startDate/endDate query params.
The live adapter currently filters to likely residential new-construction permits using permit-type heuristics so historical backfill can start before more advanced classification is added.

citrus-county.public_access: Citrus County runs on Accela Citizen Access with public anonymous search by record type and date range.
notes: Citrus County transitioned permit processing to an Accela Citizen Access portal (agency code CITRUS) on September 1, 2025.
The same Accela scraping pattern used for Polk County applies: public Building search filtered by record type and date range, detail pages for parcel/job-value metadata.
The adapter reuses the AccelaCitizenAccessAdapter base class with agency_code='CITRUS'.

lake-alfred.public_access: Public Accela Citizen Access portal (COLA). Building module searchable without login.
notes: Small city in east Polk County. Accela instance COLA. Record type Building/Residential/New/NA for new SFR permits.

winter-haven.public_access: Accela Citizen Access portal (COWH) requires authentication for Building module search.
notes: Largest city in east Polk County. Migrated from Tyler eSuite to Accela in Jan 2026. Building module requires login.

haines-city.public_access: Public iWorQ permit portal with date-range search. Non-standard column layout (no permit type column).
notes: Second largest east Polk city. iWorQ portal with unique column layout. Type filtering deferred to detail page Description field. Builders: KB Home, Lennar, DR Horton.

davenport.public_access: Public iWorQ permit portal with date-range search. 10-column layout with Description, Project Cost, Primary Contractor.
notes: High-growth I-4 corridor city. iWorQ portal with rich column layout. Builders: Highland Homes/Clayton, Trinity Family.

lake-hamilton.public_access: iWorQ portal with reCAPTCHA enforcement and no date-range search field.
notes: Very small municipality (~1500 pop). iWorQ portal blocks automated access with reCAPTCHA. No date-range search available.
